novelty_flags.label: upgrade
novelty_flags.one_line: Mechanism upgrade is only partial: Pi is presented as a common-rotation pasting channel, but Q_LP already ranges over one full common rotation across horizons, so the third channel does not yet isolate a distinct bundled mechanism; as written tier=field below novelty_target=flagship.

soundness_flags.label: Formal setup / Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The set P(P) defining Pi(Q;P) uses 'compatible with at least one full orthogonal completion' although Q is already a full orthogonal rotation; the proposal must define the pasted horizonwise object or make Pi a nonredundant distance to a precise set.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine the LP/sign relaxation so the pasting defect compares genuinely horizonwise LP response rows or per-horizon optimizers against a single common SVAR rotation; then restate Pi with an explicit equation and show L_delta4 alone does not already imply the zero set.
Add a two-shock or three-horizon worked specialization where E_w, L_delta4, and Pi can be nonzero independently, with tau_w^*(P) changing when each channel is switched off.
Upgrade Conjecture 2 from an abstract local modulus to an explicit finite-dimensional optimization program for c_w(P), with variables, constraints, and observable inputs listed.

recommended_next_step: Patch the mechanism definition of Pi so it is a genuine nonredundant channel, then rerun the upgrade review against the parent reduction.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The proposal separates exposure and cumulant-label residuals, but the claimed common-rotation pasting residual is not yet mathematically distinct from the existing full-rotation SVAR feasibility condition.
